sections.paragraph:
ῥίζα, ης, ἡ [cp. Lat. radix ‘root’]

—1. root

—a. of plant life Mt 3:10; 13:6; Mk 4:6; 11:20; Lk 3:9.

—b. in imagery of genealogical or family stock Mt 13:21; Mk 4:17; Lk 8:13; Ro 11:16-18; 1 Ti 6:10; Hb 12:15.

—2. the idea of a root as source of growth for an entire plant transfers to a secondary source of growth, shoot, scion, in imagery Ro 15:12; Rv 5:5; 22:16.
